info.title: KIN API
info.description:
KIN AI Companion Platform — We Build You A Friend

The KIN API powers Mission Control (web dashboard), Telegram/Discord/WhatsApp bots, local KIN instances, and third-party integrations. It provides:

- **Auth** — Telegram Login Widget + JWT-based sessions
- **Chat** — Multi-companion AI chat with a two-brain supervisor (Local Ollama → Groq → Anthropic/OpenAI fallback)
- **Skills Marketplace** — Browse, install, and custom-submit companion skill extensions
- **Companion Skills** — NFT-portable XP/leveling system for companion skill trees
- **Heartbeat & Recovery** — Local KIN health monitoring and snapshot-based recovery
- **Billing** — Stripe subscription checkout, portal, and webhook handling
- **Support** — AI-powered support chat with human escalation, FAQ, tickets, and feature requests

All protected routes require a JWT bearer token obtained via `POST /auth/telegram` or `POST /auth/dev-login` (development only).

bearerAuth.description: JWT access token. Obtain via `POST /auth/telegram` or `POST /auth/dev-login`. Token expires after 2 hours. Algorithm: HS256.

token.description: JWT bearer token. Valid for 2 hours.

CompanionId.description: Companion identifier. Each companion has a distinct personality and specialisation.

conversationId.description: UUID of the conversation. Reuse to maintain context.

route.description: Which inference provider handled the request.

description.example: Reviews code for bugs, style, and performance.

isPortable.description: Portable skills travel with the NFT when the companion is transferred.

TooManyRequests.description: Rate limit exceeded. Default: 100 requests/minute per IP in production.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication via Telegram Login Widget and JWT token management.

tags.name: Chat
tags.description: Real-time AI chat with companions. Uses a two-brain supervisor architecture: Local Ollama → Groq (free) → Anthropic/OpenAI (paid fallback). Includes SSE streaming and GDPR data export.

tags.name: Skills
tags.description: Companion skills marketplace. Browse built-in and community skills, toggle them per companion, and submit custom GitHub repositories for review.

tags.name: Companions
tags.description: NFT-portable companion skill accrual system. Skills level up through use and travel with the companion NFT when transferred on Solana.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: System health checks and local KIN instance heartbeat monitoring.

tags.name: Recovery
tags.description: Snapshot-based recovery for local KIN instances. Creates and restores data snapshots without deleting user data.

tags.name: Support
tags.description: AI-powered customer support chat, FAQ, ticket management, and feature voting.

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Stripe subscription management. Checkout sessions, customer portal, and webhook event processing. Companion NFT mint payments also flow through here.

get.description: Returns overall system health including database, memory, and storage checks. Returns HTTP 503 if the system is degraded.

503.description: System is degraded — one or more checks failed.

get.description: Kubernetes-style readiness check. Returns 503 if the database is unavailable.
Kubernetes-style liveness check. Always returns 200 if the process is alive.

post.description:
Verifies the Telegram Login Widget payload using HMAC-SHA256 with the bot token secret key. Creates a new user account on first login. Returns a signed JWT valid for 2 hours.

The `hash` field must be a valid Telegram login hash and the `auth_date` must be within 24 hours.

auth_date.description: Unix timestamp of authentication. Must be within 24 hours.

hash.description: HMAC-SHA256 verification hash computed by Telegram.

401.description: Invalid Telegram hash or expired auth_date.

500.description: Server misconfiguration — bot token not set.

get.description: Decodes the bearer token and returns the associated user profile. Returns `{valid: false}` for expired or malformed tokens without raising an error — safe to poll from the client.

post.description: Creates or finds a user by Telegram ID without hash verification. **Only available when `NODE_ENV=development`.** This endpoint does not exist in production. Useful for local testing and smoke tests.

telegramId.description: Any Telegram-style numeric ID to use as test identity.

firstName.description: Display name for the test user.

post.description:
Sends a user message to the specified companion and returns the AI response. Automatically creates a new conversation or continues an existing one. The two-brain supervisor tries providers in order: Local Ollama → Groq → Anthropic/OpenAI.

Messages are persisted for context continuity. The `conversationId` is a UUID — provide it on subsequent requests to maintain conversation thread context (up to 20 recent messages are included).

message.example: Can you review this Python function for edge cases?

conversationId.description: Existing conversation ID to continue. Omit to start a new conversation.

post.description:
Same as `POST /chat` but streams the response word-by-word as Server-Sent Events. The connection stays open until the full response is delivered.

Event format: `data: {content: string, done: boolean, ...metadata}`

The final event has `done: true` and includes `conversationId`, `companionId`, `route`, and `latencyMs`. On error, `done: true` is set with an `error` field.

200.description: SSE stream of companion response tokens.

schema.description: Newline-delimited SSE events. Each event is a JSON payload prefixed with `data: `.

get.description: Returns which LLM providers are configured and available. Useful for the dashboard to show which inference path will be used.
Returns all conversations, messages, and memories belonging to the authenticated user as a single JSON payload. Compliant with GDPR Article 20 (data portability).
Returns all approved skills in the marketplace. Each skill includes an `isInstalled` and `isActive` flag relative to the authenticated user. Optionally filter by category or search term.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across skill name and description.

get.description: Returns all skills installed by the authenticated user, including which companion each skill is assigned to and when it was installed.

200.description: User's installed skills with companion assignment.

post.description: Installs a skill for the first time (if not yet installed) and sets its active state. If the skill is already installed, only the `is_active` flag is toggled. On first install, the skill's global install count is incremented.

active.description: Desired active state for the skill.

companionId.description: Companion to associate the skill with. Optional.

post.description:
Submit a GitHub repository URL for custom skill review. A $4.99 review fee is charged via Stripe. The repository is reviewed by the KIN team and, if approved, published to the marketplace.

Returns the request ID and `amountCents`. Follow up with `POST /skills/request/{id}/checkout` to pay the review fee.

githubRepoUrl.description: Full GitHub repository URL. Must be a public repository.

200.description: Skill request created. Proceed to checkout.

post.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout session for the one-time $4.99 skill review fee. Returns the Stripe Checkout URL to redirect the user to. On successful payment, the webhook updates the skill request status to `paid` and triggers the review queue.

successUrl.description: URL to redirect to after payment. Defaults to the KIN dashboard.

cancelUrl.description: URL to redirect to on payment cancellation.

url.description: Redirect the user to this URL. Null if Stripe is not configured.

message.description: Present if Stripe is not configured.

get.description: Returns all skill requests submitted by the authenticated user, ordered by submission date descending.
Returns all accrued skills for a specific companion, ordered by level descending. Each skill entry includes XP, level, and portability status.

post.description: Awards experience points (XP) to a companion's skill. Automatically handles level-up when XP crosses the threshold. XP values follow the curve: [0, 100, 300, 600, 1000...] per level.

skillId.description: Skill ID to award XP to.

xpGained.description: XP to award. Capped at 100 per call.

post.description: Creates a SHA-256-hashed snapshot of a companion's portable skills. Used before NFT transfers and for IPFS/on-chain skill state attestation. Private memories and conversation history are never included.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the skill payload.

get.description: Lists skill snapshots for the authenticated user, optionally filtered by companion. Returns up to 50 most recent snapshots.

parameters.name: companionId
parameters.description: Filter snapshots to a specific companion.

post.description: Prepares a companion's portable skills for NFT transfer. Creates a transfer snapshot, logs the transfer, and—if `toUserId` is provided—replicates the portable skills to the new owner. Private memories and conversation history remain with the original owner and are never transferred.

nftMintAddress.description: Solana mint address of the companion NFT.

toUserId.description: KIN user ID of the recipient. If provided, skills are replicated to the new owner.

transferTxSig.description: Solana transaction signature for the NFT transfer (for audit log).

403.description: Forbidden — caller does not own this companion NFT.

get.description: Returns all NFT transfer events where the authenticated user is either sender or recipient. Limited to 50 most recent records.

post.description: Receives a periodic heartbeat from a local KIN instance. The local agent should POST every 30 seconds. After 3 missed beats (90 seconds), the KIN is marked offline. Upserts heartbeat state — multiple KIN IDs per user are supported.

kinId.description: Unique identifier for this KIN instance.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (ms) from the local agent.

services.description: Map of service name to health status.

get.description: Returns the current health status of the user's most recently active local KIN instance. Includes service statuses, system resource metrics, and overall health assessment.
Returns the last 100 heartbeat records for the authenticated user, ordered by time descending. Useful for trend analysis and uptime monitoring.

post.description: Creates a manual recovery snapshot. Counts conversations, messages, and memories and stores metadata for the restore point. The actual data is never duplicated — only counts and metadata are stored.

get.description: Returns all recovery snapshots for the authenticated user, ordered by creation date descending. Limited to 50 snapshots.

post.description: Restores from a recovery snapshot by resetting error counters and marking the KIN heartbeat as healthy. **No user data is ever deleted.** Conversations, memories, and messages are preserved. This operation clears the service error state so the dashboard shows a clean health status.

message.example: Recovery complete. Error counters reset. All data preserved.

post.description: Sends a message to the AI-powered KIN Support bot. The bot searches the FAQ database first, then generates a response using the inference stack if no FAQ match is found. Returns the AI response and whether a FAQ match was found.

message.example: How do I upgrade to the Pro plan?

chatId.description: Existing support chat session ID. Omit to start a new session.

faqMatched.description: True if the response was sourced from the FAQ database.

post.description: Escalates the support conversation to the human support team. Creates a high-priority support ticket, sends an alert via Slack webhook (if configured), and adds a system message to the chat thread. Returns the generated ticket ID.

chatId.description: Support chat session ID to escalate.

reason.description: Optional reason for escalation (for the support agent's context).
reason.example: Billing issue not resolved by AI

get.description: Returns the message history for a support chat session. If `chatId` is not provided, returns the most recent active session. Returns an empty message list if no active session exists.

parameters.name: chatId
parameters.description: Support chat session ID. Omit for the latest active session.

get.description: Returns FAQ entries. Supports optional search and category filtering. Used by the support chat widget and the standalone FAQ page.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across question and answer.

get.description: Returns all support tickets for the authenticated user, ordered by creation date descending. Limited to 50 tickets.

post.description: Creates a new support ticket for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns the top 50 feature requests sorted by vote count descending. Each entry includes whether the authenticated user has voted.

post.description: Creates a new feature request. The request starts with 0 votes.

title.example: Voice message support in web dashboard

description.description: Detailed description of the requested feature.
description.example: Allow users to send voice notes directly from the web chat interface.

post.description: Casts an upvote for a feature request. Each user can vote once per feature. Returns 409 if the user has already voted.

get.description: Returns the current subscription plan, status, billing period, and live usage statistics (messages today, active companions, total API calls).

post.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout session for subscription purchase. Returns the Stripe-hosted checkout URL. If `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` is not configured, returns `{url: null, message: 'Payments coming soon'}`.

priceId.description: Stripe Price ID for the subscription plan.

message.description: Present only when Stripe is not configured.

post.description:
Creates a Stripe Checkout session for a one-time companion NFT mint ($9.99). On payment success, the Stripe webhook triggers the Candy Machine mint and records NFT ownership. Users do not need any crypto knowledge — they simply pay and the NFT is minted to their wallet.

Rate limited to prevent minting abuse.

walletAddress.description: Solana wallet address to receive the minted NFT.

post.description: Creates a Stripe Customer Portal session. Allows users to manage their subscription, update payment methods, view invoices, and cancel. Requires an existing Stripe customer record — the user must have subscribed at least once.

returnUrl.description: URL to return to after leaving the portal.

post.description:
Receives and processes Stripe webhook events. Signature is verified using `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (HMAC-SHA256 with 5-minute tolerance). Handles:

- `checkout.session.completed` — Activates subscriptions and processes companion mint payments
- `customer.subscription.updated` — Syncs subscription status and period dates
- `customer.subscription.deleted` — Downgrades user to free tier

The request body must be the raw Stripe JSON payload (not parsed). Register this URL in your Stripe dashboard.

schema.description: Raw Stripe event payload. Do not transform.

400.description: Invalid Stripe signature or malformed JSON.
